q: Which of the following statements is true about potential energy?
o: It is always zero at the ground level.
It can be negative depending on the reference point.
It depends on the speed of the object.
It cannot be converted into kinetic energy.

q: Why did Mendel use true-breeding plants as parents in his crosses?
o: To grow them in similar soil condition
To get large number of plants quickly
To study environmental effects on growth of plants

q: A boy walks from one end A to the other end B of a straight 1000 m road in 2 minutes and then turns around and walks 400 m back to point C in another 2 minutes. What will be the average velocity (in m/s) of the boy in going from point A to point C?
The pH values of three solutions A, B and C are respectively 3, 7 and 12. Which of the following statements is correct?
o: Solution A and C are both acidic.
Solution C is acidic while solution A is basic.
Solution B is basic and solution C is acidic.
Solution A is acidic and solution C is basic.

q: If 100 J of work is required to move a charge of 5 C between two points, what is the potential difference between the points?
Which of the following correctly shows the relationship between displacement (u) and resistance distance (v) in the following options?
Which cell organelle is responsible for packaging and dispatching materials within or outside the cell?
What is the name of the phenomenon in which a substance, due to its nature, spreads and mixes with another substance in the following options?
Which of the following is the chemical symbol for lead?
Which of the following correctly compares the cell wall and cell membrane in plant and animal cells?
o: Both plant and animal cells have a cell wall but only plant cells have a cell membrane.
Plant cells have a rigid cell wall and cell membrane, while animal cells have a flexible cell membrane.
Neither plant nor animal cells have a cell wall, but both have a cell membrane.
Animal cells have both a rigid cell wall and a cell membrane, while plant cells have only a cell membrane.

q: The process of fission in an amoeba results in the formation of ___________.
o: two similar sized, genetically identical daughter cells
a large number of small, genetically identical daughter cells
a small daughter cell and a large mother cell that are genetically different

q: According to Ohm's law, the ratio of potential difference (V) across a conductor to the current (I) through it is constant, provided _______.
What is anode mud?
o: Insoluble impurities settle at cathode in electrolytic refining
Insoluble impurities settle at anode in electrolytic refining
Soluble impurities in solution in electrolytic refining
A solution of soil and water

q: The acceleration due to gravity on the surface of the Earth is given by:
Which of the following is an example of a decomposition reaction?
Which of the following statements regarding the need for macronutrients in plants is correct?
o: Macronutrients are not required by plants at all.
Macronutrients are needed only during flowering.
Macronutrients are required by plants in large quantities.
Macronutrients are harmful to plants if absorbed.

q: Which of the following living organisms have bones that help in body movements?
Which of the following is part of the central nervous system in humans?
The refraction of light when passing from one medium into a second medium depends on ___________.
o: the shape of the light source only
the colour of incident light (wavelength) and the properties of the second medium
the speed of the light source only

q: Which of the following is an application of ultrasound in industry?
o: Increasing the speed of sound in solids

q: By which of the following methods is reproduction carried out in Plasmodium?
Which metal is found between sodium and iron in the reactivity series?
Who gave the law of conservation of mass?

Person A walks 2/3 as fast as person B. If A starts walking from his house and reaches his office in 36 minutes, and if A walks at his own speed from his house and B starts at the same time, how much time (in minutes) will B take to reach the office?
A shopkeeper announces three successive discounts on goods. Which of the following sets of discounts will be most beneficial for a customer?
What percentage is 1/20 of 11/30? (Complete up to two decimal places.)
A bus covers a certain distance at a speed of 72 km/hr and the remaining distance at a speed of 90 km/hr. If the average speed is found, what is it?
540 litres of acid and water are mixed in the ratio 2 : 10. How many litres of acid should be added to this mixture so that the ratio of acid and water becomes 5 : 9?
The ratio of the curved surface area of a right circular cone to its total surface area is 3 : 5. If its base radius is 12.96 cm², then what will be its height? (Use π = 22/7.)

In a certain competition, two people together can complete a task in 9 days, while one of them alone takes 18 days to complete the work. If both work together, in how many days can they complete the work?
Find the largest number which, when 45, 95 and 185 are divided by it, leaves the same remainder in each case.
Find the compound interest on a sum of Rs.25,200 at an annual rate of 10% over 2 years, when the interest is compounded annually.
The average age of 42 students in a class is 14 years. If the teacher's age is also included, the average age of the group becomes 15 years. What is the teacher's age (in years)?
The average runs of a cricketer in 9 innings is 68. If he scores 130 runs in the 10th inning, then by how much (nearest whole number) will his average increase?
4 men can complete a certain work in 27 days. In how many days will 6 men complete the same work in 18 days?

The price of an item decreases by 50%, 35% and 50% over three consecutive months, but in a particular month it increases by 45%. If the original price of the item is doubled, then by what percentage has the price of the item increased? (Round your answer to two decimal places.)
Rahul's present age is equal to twice his grandfather's age divided by 2/7. 16 years later, his age will be 2/5 of his grandfather's age. Find the present age of Rahul's grandfather.
The maximum retail price of a television set is Rs.35,000. A trader gives a discount of 22% and earns a profit of 75%. What is the cost price of the television set?
A bought goods worth Rs.1,50,000 and sold them to B at a loss of 30%. Then B sold them to C at a profit of 5%. If C paid Rs.78,250 for the goods, what is the cost price of the goods for C?
